artifact_contract.audience: 2026 Boise Trails route-promotion and field-packet review
artifact_contract.downstream_decision: Can the current route packet survive an adversarial route-by-route disproof attempt without hiding human-footmile fatigue risk?
artifact_contract.not_claiming: This is not a magic global optimum proof and does not rule out a future brand-new legal anchor or field-invented route.

frame_shift.original_frame: Existing audits prove the route set because hard gates pass.
frame_shift.stronger_frame: Every route must have its own adversarial hold/gate record that attacks start choice, same-credit alternatives, bundle pressure, human footmiles, and artifact drift.
frame_shift.second_pass_challenge: A route-by-route record can still overclaim if it treats generated checks as day-of field proof, so provisional and condition-gated decisions stay explicit.
failure_stories_tested: A stale public artifact keeps an old start after the field packet is repaired.
A single-segment route escapes multi-start audit pressure.
A same-trailhead bundle looks efficient on paper but worsens runnable p75 or cue complexity.
A high-ratio mountain route is accepted only because the audit stopped at GPX validity.
A private-derived anchor repairs distance but still needs field-walkthrough promotion.
A global optimizer beats mileage but lacks parking, p75, DEM effort, or continuous cues.
frame_shift.decision: hold with explicit gates and user-confirmed Avimor access

routes.same_credit_anchor_attack: Same-credit alternatives are non-dominant in the current pack; best listed savings 0.00 mi / 0 min.
routes.bundle_boundary_global_attack: Attacked as a tiny-credit out-and-back. Same-credit review found no material accepted-anchor improvement beyond the current Miller Gulch start, and no repeat/bundle audit warning remains open.
routes.human_validity_attack: Current field packet, route review pack, repeat audit, efficiency audit, p75 timing, and DEM effort are the proof layers; this does not replace day-of field judgment.
routes.disproof_result: Held as current route; no material disproof pressure remains in current audits.
routes.what_would_disprove_later: A certified, legal, same-credit or same-day replacement with current parking/access evidence, p75/DEM effort, complete GPX/cues, and at least 0.25 miles or 10 p75 minutes saved.

routes.same_credit_anchor_attack: Same-credit alternatives are non-dominant in the current pack; best listed savings 0.10 mi / 6 min.
routes.bundle_boundary_global_attack: Attacked by merging same-trailhead Hulls cards and by substituting the old 8th/Crestline superset. The superset is worse, and same-trailhead rows are scheduling opportunities, not certified lower-footmile replacements.
routes.human_validity_attack: Current field packet, route review pack, repeat audit, efficiency audit, p75 timing, and DEM effort are the proof layers; this does not replace day-of field judgment.
routes.disproof_result: Held despite same-trailhead bundle pressure because bundling is not proven to reduce runnable cost.
routes.what_would_disprove_later: A certified same-trailhead Hulls or 8th Street bundle that proves lower total p75/on-foot burden without turning a short outing into a harder field day.

routes.same_credit_anchor_attack: No same-credit accepted alternative is recorded.
routes.bundle_boundary_global_attack: Attacked by bundling Cartwright cards and recombining Polecat/Peggy boundaries. The boundary challenge and repeat audit found no dominant generated combo with p75, DEM effort, and field-ready continuity.
routes.human_validity_attack: Current field packet, route review pack, repeat audit, efficiency audit, p75 timing, and DEM effort are the proof layers; this does not replace day-of field judgment.

routes.what_would_disprove_later: A certified Cartwright/Polecat/Peggy bundle that reduces car-to-car p75 or on-foot burden and keeps the cues usable at overlapping junctions.

routes.what_would_disprove_later: A certified, legal, same-credit or same-day replacement with current parking/access evidence, p75/DEM effort, complete GPX/cues, and at least 0.25 miles or 10 p75 minutes saved.

routes.current_start: Dry Creek / Sweet Connie roadside parking
routes.same_credit_anchor_attack: No same-credit accepted alternative is recorded.
routes.bundle_boundary_global_attack: Attacked by reviving the old Hawkins/Sweet placeholder, merging same-start Dry/Sweet cards, and searching for generated exact replacements. The accepted split removes the 36.48-mile placeholder and no lower-footmile field-ready replacement is proven.
routes.human_validity_attack: Current field packet, route review pack, repeat audit, efficiency audit, p75 timing, and DEM effort are the proof layers; this does not replace day-of field judgment.
routes.disproof_result: Held despite high human-footmile pressure because current proofs do not show a safer lower-cost replacement.
routes.what_would_disprove_later: A field-certified Dry/Sweet/Sweet Connie split or lower access anchor that preserves the exact official work and beats the accepted split by at least 0.25 miles or 10 p75 minutes without parking or cue risk.

routes.same_credit_anchor_attack: No same-credit accepted alternative is recorded.
routes.bundle_boundary_global_attack: Attacked by restoring the old Freestone/Three Bears/Curlew aggregate, shorter high-overlap variants, and boundary recombination. The old aggregate is worse; shorter candidates miss official segments; no dominant boundary/global replacement is proven.
routes.human_validity_attack: Current field packet, route review pack, repeat audit, efficiency audit, p75 timing, and DEM effort are the proof layers; this does not replace day-of field judgment.
routes.disproof_result: Held despite high human-footmile pressure because current proofs do not show a safer lower-cost replacement.
routes.what_would_disprove_later: A continuous certified Freestone/Military/Three Bears recombination that covers the same official set, keeps p75/DEM evidence, and reduces human footmiles without introducing hidden repeats or latent credit.

routes.same_credit_anchor_attack: No same-credit accepted alternative is recorded.
routes.bundle_boundary_global_attack: Attacked by bundling Cartwright cards and recombining Polecat/Peggy boundaries. The boundary challenge and repeat audit found no dominant generated combo with p75, DEM effort, and field-ready continuity.
routes.human_validity_attack: Current field packet, route review pack, repeat audit, efficiency audit, p75 timing, and DEM effort are the proof layers; this does not replace day-of field judgment.
routes.disproof_result: Held despite high human-footmile pressure because current proofs do not show a safer lower-cost replacement.
routes.what_would_disprove_later: A certified Cartwright/Polecat/Peggy bundle that reduces car-to-car p75 or on-foot burden and keeps the cues usable at overlapping junctions.

routes.same_credit_anchor_attack: No same-credit accepted alternative is recorded.
routes.bundle_boundary_global_attack: Attacked by restoring the old Freestone/Three Bears/Curlew aggregate, shorter high-overlap variants, and boundary recombination. The old aggregate is worse; shorter candidates miss official segments; no dominant boundary/global replacement is proven.

routes.same_credit_anchor_attack: No same-credit accepted alternative is recorded.
routes.bundle_boundary_global_attack: Attacked by merging same-trailhead Hulls cards and by substituting the old 8th/Crestline superset. The superset is worse, and same-trailhead rows are scheduling opportunities, not certified lower-footmile replacements.
routes.human_validity_attack: Current field packet, route review pack, repeat audit, efficiency audit, p75 timing, and DEM effort are the proof layers; this does not replace day-of field judgment.
routes.disproof_result: Held despite same-trailhead bundle pressure because bundling is not proven to reduce runnable cost.
routes.what_would_disprove_later: A certified same-trailhead Hulls or 8th Street bundle that proves lower total p75/on-foot burden without turning a short outing into a harder field day.

routes.same_credit_anchor_attack: No same-credit accepted alternative is recorded.
routes.bundle_boundary_global_attack: Attacked by merging Simplot/Pioneer cards, replacing small-credit out-and-backs, and recombining Bogus day work. High ratios are explained by lodge parking and official segments away from starts; no dominant replacement exists, but day-of road/trail/closure checks remain mandatory.
routes.human_validity_attack: Current field packet, route review pack, repeat audit, efficiency audit, p75 timing, and DEM effort are the proof layers; this does not replace day-of field judgment.
routes.disproof_result: Held against known route dominance, with live condition/access checks required near field use.
routes.what_would_disprove_later: A certified Bogus-specific route with legal parking, current access, p75/DEM effort, complete GPX/cues, and same official credit that materially reduces on-foot or p75 cost; or a current closure/access finding that invalidates the lodge start.

routes.same_credit_anchor_attack: No same-credit accepted alternative is recorded.
routes.bundle_boundary_global_attack: Attacked as a late-added H1 correction, artifact-drift risk, public-source access ambiguity, and user-reviewed access claim. Current field packet/map data agree on H1, no route-review dominance remains, and the user confirmed Avimor access. The public-only source layer remains ambiguous because the Avimor owner page frames trail access as resident access, but the private field-use access gate is resolved by user-reviewed evidence.
routes.human_validity_attack: Current field packet, route review pack, repeat audit, efficiency audit, p75 timing, DEM effort, and user-reviewed Avimor access are the proof layers. This still requires normal day-of signage/condition judgment before field execution.
routes.disproof_result: Held as current route. The public-source ambiguity is documented, but user-confirmed Avimor access restores H1 to accepted-current for the private field packet.
routes.what_would_disprove_later: A later access change, Avimor/BTC restriction, current signage conflict, or certified legal same-credit replacement with at least 0.25 miles or 10 p75 minutes saved would disprove this hold.
public_source_evidence: https://www.avimor.com/trails-and-outdoors says trails are open for Avimor residents and describes resident-only annual permits for some trail uses.
Current H1 start justification relies on OSM Overpass amenity parking plus AllTrails Spring Valley Creek evidence.
User confirmed Avimor access for this field plan on 2026-05-16.

limitations: Does not prove a brand-new legal anchor or hand-drawn GPX cannot beat the current packet later.
Does not replace current closure, mud, heat, water, snow, road, or parking checks before field execution.
Does not remove provisional field-walkthrough status from accepted replacement routes.
Does not include raw private coordinates, raw activity ids, or private GPS traces.

Public-source reevaluation on 2026-05-16 found Avimor public-owner language ambiguous for H1; user-provided access confirmation resolves the private field-use gate but does not turn OSM/AllTrails into public landowner proof.
